supportCards.title: First periods, explained gently
supportCards.body: Simple answers for young users and caregivers, with age-appropriate language and no shame.

supportCards.title: Care routines that feel practical
supportCards.body: Menstrual hygiene, personal hygiene, and intimate care guidance that is easy to act on.

supportCards.title: Health education with empathy
supportCards.body: Trusted information that makes room for questions, curiosity, and privacy.

pillars: Education designed for rural and semi-urban communities

journeySteps.title: Choose the right language
journeySteps.body: Users start with the language they understand best, so the experience feels familiar from the first screen.

journeySteps.title: Ask a question or open a lesson
journeySteps.body: The interface supports casual conversation as well as structured education modules for deeper learning.

journeySteps.title: Save progress and continue later
journeySteps.body: The product flow is designed to support repeat visits, progress tracking, and future personalization.

chatMessages.text: I am nervous about my first period. What should I know?
That is completely okay. I can explain the signs, what to keep ready, and how to stay comfortable without overwhelming you.
If you want, I can also show a simple checklist or help you talk to a trusted adult.

quickPrompts: How do I stay clean safely?
How do I talk to my mother?

journeyAudiences.title: First-time learner
journeyAudiences.body: Start with simple explanations, age-appropriate terms, and a calm tone that reduces anxiety.

journeyAudiences.title: Caregiver support
journeyAudiences.body: Get practical guidance for answering questions clearly and compassionately at home.

journeyAudiences.title: Returning learner
journeyAudiences.body: Revisit topics, track what you have learned, and continue from the last lesson or chat.

hero.title: Support that feels warm, clear, and quietly confident.
hero.description: Sakhi AI helps girls and women learn about periods, hygiene, puberty, nutrition, mental wellbeing, and emergency awareness in language that feels safe and easy to trust.

liveGuidance.title: Ask softly. Learn clearly.
liveGuidance.description: The interface is designed to feel calm, respectful, and safe for first-time users, caregivers, and younger learners alike.

card1.title: Designed for trust
card1.body: Clear explanations, privacy-aware UX, and a tone that never feels rushed or dismissive.

card2.title: Accessible by default
card2.body: Large type, gentle contrast, and layout choices that work smoothly across screen sizes.

chatSection.title: A calm conversation that teaches without overwhelming.
chatSection.description: The first interaction should feel reassuring, especially for users who are shy, anxious, or asking for the first time.

supportSection.title: A gentle foundation for the first version.
supportSection.description: The first frontend experience should make the platform feel calm, trustworthy, and ready for future learning flows.

journeySection.title: A simple path from curiosity to confidence.
journeySection.description: The navigation should help users understand where to start, how to learn, and how to return later without friction.

languagesSection.title: Multilingual by design, not as an afterthought.
languagesSection.description: The app should make users feel at home in the language they choose first, while keeping content consistent and easy to extend later.
languagesSection.whyItMattersText: Language is part of safety. When users can understand the UI immediately, they can focus on learning instead of translating.

card1.title: Readable on mobile
card1.body: Responsive sections keep text comfortable to scan even on smaller screens.

card2.title: Extendable later
card2.body: The structure is ready for localized content and additional regional languages.

safetySection.title: Helpful guidance without crossing into medical advice.
safetySection.description: The interface should make the platform feel supportive while preserving clear boundaries around education and professional care.

Chat.welcomeSubtitle: महिलाओं के स्वास्थ्य के बारे में सीखने के लिए आपकी सुरक्षित जगह। कुछ भी पूछें — कोई सवाल छोटा या शर्मनाक नहीं है।
Chat.disclaimer: सखी AI केवल शिक्षा के लिए है। चिकित्सा सलाह के लिए हमेशा डॉक्टर से संपर्क करें।
quickPrompts: आयरन युक्त खाद्य पदार्थ कौन से हैं?

demoReplies.default: यह बहुत अच्छा सवाल है! मैं आपको अपने शरीर और स्वास्थ्य को समझने में मदद करने के लिए यहाँ हूँ। मैं इसे सरल और स्पष्ट तरीके से समझाऊँगी। याद रखें, गलत सवाल नहीं होते — पूछकर आप बहुत अच्छा कर रही हैं। 💛
demoReplies.cramp: मासिक दर्द इसलिए होता है क्योंकि गर्भाशय अपनी परत को हटाने के लिए सिकुड़ता है। यह बिल्कुल सामान्य है! गर्म पानी की शिशी, हल्की स्ट्रेचिंग या हाइड्रेटेड रहने से मदद मिल सकती है। अगर दर्द बहुत ज़्यादा है, तो डॉक्टर से बात करना अच्छा रहेगा। 🌸
demoReplies.cycle: मासिक चक्र वह मासिक प्रक्रिया है जिससे आपका शरीर गर्भधारण की संभावना के लिए तैयार होता है। यह आमतौर पर 21-35 दिन तक चलता है। मासिक, फॉलिक्युलर, ओव्यूलेशन और ल्यूटियल — हर चरण महत्वपूर्ण है। यह आपके शरीर की प्राकृतिक लय है! 💪
demoReplies.hygiene: पीरियड के दौरान अच्छी स्वच्छता सरल है: पैड या टैम्पन हर 4-6 घंटे में बदलें, बाहरी क्षेत्र को हल्के साबुन और पानी से धोएं, और साफ़, हवादार अंडरवियर पहनें। सबसे महत्वपूर्ण — अपने शरीर के साथ कोमल रहें, यह कड़ी मेहनत कर रहा है! 🌿

items.question: What is Sakhi AI?
items.answer: Sakhi AI is a multilingual women's health education platform designed to provide safe, accurate, and culturally sensitive information about periods, hygiene, puberty, nutrition, mental wellbeing, and more. It's like having a trusted elder sister who explains things without judgment.

items.question: Is Sakhi AI a substitute for medical advice?
items.answer: No. Sakhi AI is for educational purposes only. While we provide accurate health information, we cannot replace professional medical advice. Always consult a doctor or healthcare provider for medical concerns, especially for persistent symptoms or emergencies.

items.question: What age group is Sakhi AI for?
items.answer: Sakhi AI is designed for girls (10-13 years), teenagers (14-18 years), women (18+), mothers, and caregivers. The content adapts to different age groups, using age-appropriate language and explanations.

items.question: What languages are supported?
items.answer: Currently, Sakhi AI supports English, Hindi, Bengali, Marathi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Gujarati, Punjabi, and Odia. We're continuously working to add more regional languages to make health education accessible to everyone.

items.question: Is my health information private?
items.answer: Yes, your privacy is very important to us. We do not share your personal health information with third parties. We recommend using a personal device and clearing browser history after reading sensitive topics, especially if you use a shared device.

items.question: What should I know about my first period?
items.answer: Your first period is a normal part of growing up. It usually happens between ages 10-15, but everyone is different. Signs include breast development, vaginal discharge, and growth spurts. Keep a small kit with pads, clean underwear, and wipes. Talk to a trusted adult if you have questions.

items.question: How do I delete my account?
items.answer: To delete your account, go to Settings and look for the account management options. You can also contact our support team at hello@sakhi.ai for assistance. Note that deleting your account will remove your learning progress and chat history.

items.question: Can I use Sakhi AI offline?
items.answer: Currently, Sakhi AI requires an internet connection to access lessons and chat features. We're working on offline capabilities for future updates so you can learn even without connectivity.

items.question: What if I have a medical emergency?
items.answer: If you have a medical emergency, please contact emergency services immediately or go to the nearest hospital. Sakhi AI is not equipped to handle emergencies. For urgent health concerns, always seek professional medical help right away.

items.question: How accurate is the health information?
items.answer: Our content is reviewed by health educators and follows WHO guidelines for women's health education. We prioritize scientific accuracy while keeping explanations simple and easy to understand. We update our content regularly based on new health guidelines.

items.question: Can parents use Sakhi AI?
items.answer: Yes, parents and caregivers are welcome to use Sakhi AI to learn how to have conversations about health with their children. We provide guidance on answering questions clearly and compassionately, creating a supportive environment at home.

items.question: How do I report a technical issue?
items.answer: If you encounter any technical problems, please contact our support team at hello@sakhi.ai with details about the issue, including what you were trying to do and any error messages you saw. We'll work to resolve it as quickly as possible.
